entry.title: Replacement of Core Tool functionalities by functionalities in Cockpit Fiori apps as of SAP CC 2025
entry.content:
IMPORTANT NOTICE

Dear SAP BRIM Community, as of the next major release of SAP Convergent Charging, which will be released in Q4 2025, some Core Tool functionalities will be gradually replaced by functionalities available in Cockpit Fiori apps.

The functionalities provided in Fiori apps will cover 100% of the documented scope for the Core Tool. The replacement strategy consists in replacing functionalities, user role by user role. For each user role, a dedicated SAP Note will be published detailing the corresponding functionalities available in the Cockpit.

As an example, the first user role being considered is the User Administrator role, for which the functionalities will not be available in the Core Tool any longer. The User Administrator role will then use the dedicated Fiori apps available in the Cockpit:

  • Manage User Accounts
  • Manage Permissions

 There are multiple reasons why we are making this critical step:

Better User Experience

With Fiori apps available in the Cockpit, we intend to enhance the user experience that is aligned with SAP’s strategy and provides consistency across SAP products, and in SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management in particular.

AI-assisted Functionalities

AI-assisted functionalities, which will be added over time to SAP Convergent Charging in private cloud, will only be available through the Cockpit and its Fiori apps and won’t be available in the Core Tool.

For detailed information on the planned innovations, please visit SAP Road Map Explorer at roadmaps.sap.com.

Easier Deployment

At the end of the day, users won’t have to install Core Tool on their desktop/laptop but will only use their Web browser to interact with SAP Convergent Charging.

If you have any feedback, please leave a note on this blog page.

entry.title: Re: Change the inspection stock of material XXXXXXX in QM...
entry.content:
Hello @Shimpei2 

The message QA495 is described in the note 86638 - Error: QA495 during Inventory management Posting. Briefly, it's purpose is to keep QM and MM stock data consistent.

As your custom movement types are copies of physical inventory postings, see the note 83954 - Physical inventory of inspection stock about possible solution. You need to reflect in QM inspection lots stock level changes posted in MIGO.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: RF Framework split Warehouse Task
entry.content:
When processing a warehouse task via the RF framework, the system automatically divides the task into 2 individual tasks:
1. Outbound booking
2. Inbound booking

For this reason, we have to double-confirm each task during production deployment via the RF Framework.
We use eEWM in conjunction with the SAP Public Cloud.

Unfortunately, the whole thing creates an increased additional effort in the provision of materials.

I hope someone here can help me with this problem.
Thank you very much

entry.title: Re: How to automate the determination of partner bank account when creating invoices?
entry.content: Hi Vedant, During a business transaction, payments can be made into different bank accounts owned by the supplier, depending on the company code. However, inappropriate payee bank account determination can happen. To avoid this case, Partner Bank Type is provided to identify a bank account and its company code. Previously during the invoice creation process, the partner bank type information must be manually entered into the document via the field “Partner Bank Type”. How to save the repetitive manual effort for accounts payable accountants in this step? Substituting “Partner Bank Type” with other fields (e.g. Supplier ID) can solve this problem.

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 2
entry.content:
Hi @devbot 

regarding 1.I try to clarify the terminology a bit.
I assume with "library app" you mean the HTML5 Application which contains the UI5 bundle of type library (declared in the manifest). 
Any UI5 package can define different types in its manifest.

  • An "application" is something which you can start and run as an app.
  • A component is a reusable part which you can embed into an application. It is almost an app, but would not be started directly.
  • A library contains controls and reuse code and is not runnable independently as an app.

However it is possibly to embed them within others in a subfolder. E.g. in an HTML5 Application you can have

  • an application in the root folder and components in subfolders.
  • a library in the root folder which embeds components in a sub folder.
  • However embedded applications in a library is not a usual pattern (although it is possible).

HTML Repository does not care about these types. It only knows HTML5 Applications which are defined by the manifests in the root folder. The name of the HTML5 App is derived from the sap.app/id. It does not know about the embedded manifest files in sub folder. It will list all known HTML5 applications in the cockpit and provide you a link to the app regardless of the type. 
Any HTML5 app can provide html pages to run standalone and define an entry page which is opened by default. All further routing to backend services can be configured in xs-app.json and works as long as you properly use relative paths as explained in the blog.

I.e. you can add an index.html to the HTML5 App which contains the library in the root and open it. It just does not make sense, because a library is not intended to be started as an application. You would have to put an app inside and start this. 

Regarding 2. it depends how you connect app1 to the library (or whatever it is). If it is an app 2 app navigation in the launchpad, you would pass parameters in the navigation intend (however you can't navigate to a library because a library can't run on its own. Outside of the launchpad you would have to implement your own app2app navigation.
If it is a component which you embed, there are different ways to have a communication which I can't describe fully. Possibilities are start-up parameters, providing component interfaces, using event bus, using the propagated models of the main app,...
If it is a library, the app would call its reusable methods or controls and set the control properties as needed.

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: In AMDP, is it possible to use a SEQUENCE object? (nextval)
entry.content:
I'd like to do a SELECT query in an AMDP method with one column being a number starting at 1, incremented by 1, without gaps.

As ROW_NUMBER is not guaranteed to avoid gaps, I guess I should use a SEQUENCE HANA object that I reset to 1 before running the query. For using a sequence, I need to use HANA SQL, so either I use native SQL, or I use a CDS table function and AMDP.

With native SQL, it works:

REPORT.
DATA dummy_column TYPE c LENGTH 1.
DATA autoincrement TYPE i.
DATA error TYPE REF TO cx_root.

TRY.
    EXEC SQL.
      drop sequence zmysequence;
    ENDEXEC.
  CATCH cx_sy_native_sql_error INTO error ##NO_HANDLER ##NEEDED.
ENDTRY.

TRY.
    EXEC SQL.
      create sequence zmysequence start with 1 increment by 1;
    ENDEXEC.
  CATCH cx_sy_native_sql_error INTO error ##NO_HANDLER ##NEEDED.
ENDTRY.

TRY.
    EXEC SQL PERFORMING test.
      select dummy_column, 
             zmysequence.nextval autoincrement
          from ( select 'A' dummy_column from dummy
                 union select 'B' dummy_column from dummy )
          into :dummy_column, :autoincrement
    ENDEXEC.
  CATCH cx_sy_native_sql_error INTO error ##NO_HANDLER ##NEEDED.
ENDTRY.

ASSERT 1 = 1. " dummy statement for setting a breakpoint (if debug needed)

FORM test.
  WRITE : / dummy_column, autoincrement.
ENDFORM.

If now I try with AMDP, it doesn't allow sequences:

Error without USING:

"ZMYSEQUENCE" is unknown. ABAP objects and DDIC objects must be declared in the USING clause of the method. Local names must start with ":" here.

Error with USING:

"ZMYSEQUENCE" is invalid. Only transparent database tables, views, or AMDP methods can be specified after USING.

Is there a way to define an autoincrement with AMDP?

entry.title: How to check if Group reporting have been activated or not in S/4 HANA system
entry.content:
Backgroud:
As you might know, the solution of "SAP Group reporting"(FIN-CS) was developed based on EC-CS, there are a lot of shared tables and programs.
In other words,  Parallel Usage of EC-CS and SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting is a big challenge. You can either use EC-CS or FIN-CS.
Only in some specific situation, you can have both be used in the system, for more details, please check below SAP Note:
2833748 - SAP S/4HANA OP ( "on premise" ) - Transition from EC-CS to SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting

In order to use "SAP Group reporting"(FIN-CS)，You must activated the Group reporting by activating the Best Practice scope item XX_1SG_OP or running Initialize Settings in an EC-CS client, which contains EC-CS configuration without any transaction data in database table ECMCT. And after that, EC-CS becomes unusable! 

For the Group Reporting function activation, you may refer to FAQ SAP Note 2659672 - FAQ About SAP S/4HANA Finance for Group Reporting (On Premise)
Please check chapter 1.1 and 1.21 for this FAQ SAP note. It refers to the Admin Guide how to proceed with Best Practice activation in general.
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/4cef93946a0b48ec89533b3c34443b85/17d958a88d244ee293aed687f9bfe37f.html?locale=en-US
It also contains a document:
How To Install Best Practices Content 1SG_1809_V1.pdf and How To Install Best Practices Content 1SG_V4.pdf(this is valid for S/4 HANA 1909 and later release)

Check if Group reporting activated or not:
1) go to SE16N, check the field below in table TF001,
Starting Year for LIs in SAP S/4HANA    -->  it means group report have been activated
From Year for New Group Reporting Logic  -> it means new group reporting have been activated
From Year f. Group Report. Prep. Ledger -> it means GRPL have been used from this year
2) check in table TF007 - SAP Consolidation: System Status in S/4HANA 
there should be one entry with "System Status of S/4 HANA Consolidation" = X

Others:
For more information about above fields, please check the F1 help of it like below:
------------------------------
Starting Year for LIs in SAP S/4HANA 

Definition

Business year from which the system maintains the line items in Consolidation in SAP S/4 HANA structures.

Use

Before the start of the effective from fiscal year, the system behaves like EC-CS; at the start of the effective from fiscal year, the system behaves like SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting.

At the beginning of the effective from fiscal year, the system only stores the transactional data as line items, this means there is no aggregation of documents to the summary data.

The line items in the SAP S/4 HANA structures enable the processing and evaluation of consolidation at a high level of detail.

Dependencies

The transactional data before the effective from fiscal year remains unchanged in the consolidation structures, this means that there is no conversion of the legacy data to the SAP S/4 HANA structures.

During the execution of the balance carryforward in the effective from fiscal year, the system reads the summary data of the previous year from the structures in consolidation and carries forward this data as line Items to the structures in S/4 HANA to the period zero of the effective year.

Not specifying the effective from fiscal year is equivalent to 9999, that is, in this case the system continues to use the summary data and line items in consolidation, but not the detailed line items in consolidation in SAP S/4 HANA.

After you have specified the effective from fiscal year, you can no longer change it.
------------------------------

From Year for New Group Reporting Logic

Short Text

From Year for New Group Reporting Logic in SAP S/4HANA

Definition

From year for new logic for group reports in SAP S/4HANA

Use

If the from year is not initial, the new logic for group reports in SAP S/4HANA is active in the from year and in later fiscal years. This has consequences if the fiscal year that you set in global settings is not earlier than the from year:

  • In SAP S/4HANA, there are no hierarchies for consolidation groups with the new group reporting logic; instead, such consolidation groups contain a flat list of consolidation units. You maintain the structure of consolidation groups in group reporting in SAP S/4HANA in the application Manage Group Structure.
  • The report logic rates the assignment of the consolidation units (and also the assignment of partner units when eliminating pairs) for the current consolidation group for which you execute the report to decide whether a data record is displayed in the report. However, the reporting logic does not evaluate the attributes of the consolidation units (consolidation intervals, inclusion types) that depend on the consolidation group. The reports thereby also show data records before the first consolidation or after the divestiture accounting, as well as data records for consolidation units, which are consolidated using the equity method.
  • The tasks of the type Consolidation Group Change post additional documents for the periodic cancellation of data records before the first consolidation or after divestiture accounting, as well as of data records in consolidation units using the equity method.

Dependencies

The from year for the new group logic must not be earlier than the from year for line items in SAP S/4HANA.

The from year for the new group logic is not ready for input because this setting is made exclusively by SAP Support. To reconcile these activities, you must open a customer message on SAP Service Marketplace on the FIN-CS-COR component.
------------------------------
From Year f. Group Report. Prep. Ledger

Short Text

From Year for Group Reporting Preparation Ledger

Definition

From year for the integration of Financial Accounting and Group Reporting via preparation ledger

Use

If you specify a non-initial fiscal year for the from year for group reporting preparation ledger, you can use the enhanced functionality for the integration of Financial Accounting and Group Reporting in this from year and in following years. To do this, you must maintain additional settings:

  • In process step Define Preparation Ledger, specify the Financial Accounting ledger to which the system is to post information relevant for consolidation (Group Reporting) to Financial Accounting. You must assign a Group Reporting consolidated chart of accounts to each of these Financial Accounting preparation ledgers. Additionally, you can specify special versions for line item assignment and line item attributes (optional).
  • In process step Define Versions, you assign the Group reporting preparation ledger and the source for group currency key figure to a consolidation version in which you want to use the enhanced functionality for integration. Assignment of the source for group currency key figure to the master data of the consolidation units is therefore obsolete.

Dependencies

You can only set the from year for group reporting preparation ledger if the both the following from years are non-initial:

  • From year for line items in SAP S/4HANA
  • From year for the new group reporting logic in SAP S/4HANA

The from year for group reporting preparation ledger must not be earlier than the from year for the new group reporting logic in SAP S/4HANA.
------------------------------

entry.title: Accessing Product Description in Value Help Using sap/api_product/srvd_a2x/sap/product/0002 API
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm working on an SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming) project where I need to access product descriptions in a value help for a product using the OData API https://{host}:{port}/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_product/srvd_a2x/sap/product/0002. However, I'm encountering an issue when implementing the search help for the product description, and I need some guidance.

To access the external API I've defined an index.cds file under /srv/external. In this file I have included the following entity projects:

namespace external;

@readonly
entity Product                               as
    projection on PRODUCT_0002.Product {
        key Product,
            BaseUnit,
            _ProductDescription  : redirected to ProductDescription,
    }

@readonly
entity ProductDescription                         as
    projection on PRODUCT_0002.ProductDescription {
        key Product,
        key Language,
            ProductDescription,
    }

In my service definition I've included the project following entities:

using {external as externalServices} from './external';

    entity Product                as projection on externalServices.Product;
    entity ProductDescription     as projection on externalServices.ProductDescription;

In the annotation for the fiori application I have included:

annotate service.x with {
    product @(
        Common.Label : '{i18n>product}',
        Common.ValueList : {
            $Type : 'Common.ValueListType',
            CollectionPath : 'ProductDescription',
            Parameters : [
                {
                    $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
                    LocalDataProperty : product_Product,
                    ValueListProperty : 'Product',
                },
            ],
        },
        Common.ValueListWithFixedValues : false,
    )
};

In the console I get the following error:

    message: 'Error during request to remote service: Unspecified provider error occurred. See Error Context and Call Stack.',
    code: 'ERR_BAD_REQUEST',
    config: {
      timeout: 0,
      xsrfCookieName: 'XSRF-TOKEN',
      xsrfHeaderName: 'X-XSRF-TOKEN',
      maxContentLength: -1,
      maxBodyLength: -1,
      method: 'get',
      baseURL: 'https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_product/srvd_a2x/sap/product/0002',
      proxy: false,
      url: "/Product(Product='undefined')?$expand=_ProductDescription($filter=Language%20eq%20'undefined')",
      data: undefined
    },

What am I missing here?

Thank you in advance for your time!

Nicolaas

entry.title: Can Output Management be used for custom forms?
entry.content:
I'm able to put a completely custom form generated PDF into a Print Queue on S/4HANA Public Edition. That's a great start, but it does not enable users to reprint anything.

Output Management does contain that functionality, but as far as I can see that's only available for standard SAP form types. Nor is there an API or Business Object with which I can interact with Output Management. If I interpret the app correctly, Output Management is the step before actual output. From there, a document can be mailed, transferred to a different system or printed. In the latter case, it would then end up in the corresponding Print Queue.

An alternative would be to create a custom Fiori app for it, but we would of course prefer to stick to as much standard apps and functionality as possible.

entry.title: Quarterly Release Highlights: SAP BTP Security and Identity & Access Management
entry.content:
In the first quarter of 2025, we released several new features and enhancements for SAP Cloud Identity Services. Let’s take a closer look!

SAP Cloud Identity Services allows tenant administrators to create and edit application-specific groups via the administration console. While this feature was released at the end of 2024, we added token attribute configuration capabilities in 2025. You can now specify how the local user attributes, configured to be collected by the registration and upgrade forms, are sent to the application. Read Configuring User Attributes from the Identity Directory for detailed information.

You can now enable identity federation for an application to override the identity federation settings on the configured corporate identity provider for the application. For more information, check Configure Identity Federation for Applications.

We added automatic renewal of expired SAML signing certificates. Identity Authentication can automatically update the expired encryption certificate as well as the SAML 2.0 certificate during the first sign-in attempt that fails due to the expired certificate. You can find details in our SAP Help Portal documentation: Configure SAML 2.0 Service Provider.

We extended the Identity Directory service to trigger immediate provisioning of application-specific groups. This enables companies to adopt our reference architecture for cloud-leading identity lifecycles and accelerate adoption of SAP cloud solutions. It also reduces efforts for point-to-point connections. The SAP Help Portal offers additional information.

You can now download and view the details of all entities updated by a provisioning job. For more information, see Manage Provisioning Job Logs and List of Properties.

Want to stay up to date on our services? Join our SAP BTP Security and SAP Cloud Identity Services communities!

entry.title: Re: How to input different GI date in 1 delivery note number
entry.content:
Hello,

goods issue date is a header parameter in the outbound delivery, so it cannot be different for each item. If you want to have different GI dates per items, you need to create separate deliveries (you can update the dates in a sales order or directly in the delivery). 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: When to Update Node.js Based on the CAP Node.js Version?
entry.content:
CAP itself supports all LTS versions of Node which are not yet EOL. This means with CAP v9 we drop support for Node 18 and soon also add support for Node 24 once it reaches Active LTS.

Node itself states (https://nodejs.org/en/about/previous-releases) that Production applications should only use Active LTS or Maintenance LTS releases.

I personally would use the Active LTS to benefit from the newest features of Node.js.

entry.title: SAP Backbone connectivity
entry.content:
when we configure the backbone connectivity, what all data will be transferred to SAP. we need the data classification in detail. Also how we can make sure that data transfer will be secure. what all protocol will be used. as our system will be connected to SAP, how SAP make sure that there is no security breach.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Backbone connectivity
entry.content:
Dear customer,

The support backbone is the infrastructure that we use to provide you with technical support. Your systems connect to the support backbone to exchange information, such as support incident data, maintenance planner data, and SAP Early Watch Alert data.

For your question how to make sure that data transfer will be secure and how SAP make sure that there is no security breach:

I would recommend refer to below documents according to your SP level for details:

SAP Support Backbone Update Checklists | SAP Help Portal

  • The HTTPS protocol is now used instead of RFC. Most functions in SAP Solution Manager use either BSP or Web Dynpro technologies, which are both based on the HTTP or HTTPS protocols. The infrastructure required to handle HTTP and HTTPS requests using work processes in the SAP system is provided by the Internet Communication Framework (ICF). The ICF also handles Internet communication between systems that use the standard protocols HTTP, HTTPS, and SMTP. To secure communication with a Web browser, you do not require any additional SAP program libraries. The only condition is that your system platform is able to access the Internet, meaning that you have the greatest amount of flexibility when responding to various communication requirements.  
  • A technical communication user handles the data transfer instead of generic users. Technical communication users allow machine-to-machine communication and data exchange. Only super administrators and user administrators have access to the Technical Users app, where they can create, activate, and delete technical communication users.
  • There is no generic inbound interface.
  • Applications send data asynchronously unless the data is sent manually. 

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: 关于 SAP 公有云（S/4HANA Cloud）的销售数据锁定
entry.content:
About Sales Data Locking in SAP Public Cloud (S/4HANA Cloud) Here's the English translation:

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, how can you systematically control the creation and modification of sales documents (such as VA01/VA02/VF01, and so on) during a monthly sales summary (for example, from 1 to 3 days a month)? (If there are still users creating or modifying sales data (such as orders, invoices) during the rollup period (such as the 1st to 3rd of the following month), this can lead to inaccurate aggregation results.)You want the system to lock the creation and modification of sales data automatically during aggregation to avoid human interference. The lock time (such as 1~3) for accounting closing sales data and the lock time for accounting documents (e.g. after 5 days) need to be controlled separately by the finance department within 5 business days of the following month and cannot affect normal financial postings.

What function do you need to use in the standard system to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: Creating IBP time periods including weights
entry.content:
Hi,

Below are the steps you can follow:

1) Manually extend the End Date of time profile. 2) Activate the time profile 3) Go to Application job app select the template "Create Time Periods for Time Profiles" and give the required details. 4) Go to Data integration (DI) app - download the template of your time periods by selecting  "with Existing Time Periods" under the prefill template. 5) In the weekweight column, apply the excel formula  6) Save it and Reupload the same template in DI app.

 

Regards,

Sonal S

entry.title: Re: INSERT FROM SELECT - The expression that contains "WA...
entry.content:
The warning is because of:

  1. the source column BKPF-WAERS is not defined with the words NOT NULL, hence theoretically some values could be NULL,
  2. the target column ZTABLE-WAERS is defined with NOT NULL,
  3. so it might lead to an exception at runtime if BKPF-WAERS is null.

Hence, COALESCE is a good solution to avoid the exception to define a value instead of the NULL value:

INSERT ztable FROM ( SELECT coalesce( bkpf~waers , ' ' ), ... FROM zcview ).

entry.title: Re: Customer Clearing Open Items using Automatic Clearing...
entry.content: Note also In Automatic clearing APP, all items of a group are considered and no item is excluded. In this case, even there is a subgroup with balance zero, this subgroup cannot be cleared because the balance of the whole group is not zero.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - Message Mapping - addContextChange in UDF is ignored
entry.content:
Hi expterts,

I am trying to merge several single values into one structure using an UDF with the addContextChange function.
Unfortunately it does not work as expected. 

My source structure look similar to this all with occurence 0..1:
[...]
costCenter1 
costCenter2
costCenter3
percentage1
percantage2
percentage3
[...]

Inside the target message I would like to group them in a structure like this:
[...]
costDistribution (0..*)
- costCenter (1..1)
- percentage (1..1)
[...]

To map the values I am using an UDF function:
def void costDistribution(String[] costCenter1, String[] costCenter2, String[] costCenter3, String[] percentage1, String[] percentage2, String[] percentage3, Output parentNode, Output costCenters, Output percentages, MappingContext context){ ... }

For test purposes I tried this with static values to get a feeling for the context change function and tried this:
parentNode.addValue("004025235")
costCenters.addValue("004025235")
percentages.addValue("80")

parentNode.addContextChange()
costCenters.addContextChange()
percentages.addContextChange()

parentNode.addValue("004025240")
costCenters.addValue("004025240")
percentages.addValue("20")

My expection in the target would be to have several costDistribution nodes with the corresponding values like this:
[...]
costDistribution
- costCenter 004025235
- percentage 80
costDistribution
- costCenter004025240
- percentage 20
[...]

In Reality the first block is generated. May someone explain me how I need to use the contextChange function to achieve the wanted structure?
Thank you all in advance.

Best regards

entry.title: Sales Innovation with SAP S/4HANA, Business AI, and User Experience: A Practical Approach
entry.content:
From the Point of View of a Sales Representative, Optimizing Sales with SAP S/4HANA. Salespeople in today's fast-paced companies must multitask all the time to keep up with the demands of their jobs, including nurturing connections with customers, negotiating and completing deals, and monitoring key performance indicators. With the help of business AI, the digital assistant Joule, and the new SAP S/4HANA system, sales representatives are now able to accomplish more in less time. In this article, we'll look at how SAP S/4HANA boosts sales rep efficiency, solves common business problems, and puts SAP ahead of the competition.

The Routine of a Salesperson Using SAP S/4HANA

Just picture yourself as a salesperson beginning each day with a synopsis of the most pressing matters that need your attention right away. This is made very easy and feasible using SAP S/4HANA. Important tasks, forthcoming meetings, and awaiting approvals are highlighted on a personalized dashboard made possible by the platform's user-friendly design. This clear view of your priorities allows you to start each day on a positive note.

Harnessing AI for Your Company

Integrating with advanced business AI is one of SAP S/4HANA's distinguishing characteristics. This technology helps sales representatives make informed decisions by analysing large volumes of data and providing actionable insights. To illustrate the point, AI can determine which leads have the best chance of converting, so you can direct your attention to the most promising ones. Furthermore, it can detect patterns and trends in consumer behaviour, letting you adjust your sales tactics appropriately.

Navigating SAP S/4HANA with Ease

The intuitive design of SAP S/4HANA makes navigating the system a joy. There is less of a learning curve for new users on the site because of how it is designed. With SAP S/4HANA, you can easily update customer information, generate sales reports, and track your performance KPIs. Focus on what matters most - creating relationships and closing business - with the help of the effortless navigation that saves time and boosts productivity.

Have a look at Joule: Your Personal Digital AI Assistant
The digital AI assistant Joule, which is a part of SAP S/4HANA, is going to revolutionize the way sales personnel do their jobs. When you use Joule, it's like having your own personal assistant that streamlines your work process. Would you like to set up a meeting? No problem for Joule. Are you in search of the most recent sales data? Get up-to-the-minute information with Joule. You can focus on more strategic endeavors with the time you gain from using Joule to automate mundane chores.

Dealing with Problems Facing the Company
Managing client connections, monitoring sales success, and keeping up with market developments are a few of the common challenges that sales professionals encounter. Let’s follow the system and navigate to understand this better way. Here Jessica Walker acts as a sales representative. Let's start the demo - 

I am Jessica Walker, a sales representative, and I commence my day by examining tasks and notifications that want prompt attention.

Fig 1 Task & Situations

 Rather than navigating multiple reports and dashboards, I review "My Home" for a crisp overview of urgent tasks and notifications. Thereby addressing the following task.

- Approving  a quotation that requires immediate attention.
- Examine situation notifications regarding the uploaded incomplete PDF and assess the deficient data.

Fig 2 Incomplete SO

Clicking on notifications directs me to the impacted sales order. The status indicates "Data Incomplete." A notification bell captures my attention.

Fig 3: Item Level SO error

I promptly review the warning and discover that the system has already diagnosed the issue and suggested a solution.

Fig 4 : AI guided Explaination

Upon the accurate update of data, the system permits a side-by-side display for verification.

Fig 5: PDF comparisonThe data has been updated, and save it for subsequent processing.

Fig 6: Udate Missing data

Then I scroll down and observe many "Insight Business Cards." One of them is "Sales Order Blocked." It is also displaying an itemized list of sales orders. As a sales representative, I am accountable for the seamless execution of the sales cycle. I opt to examine a single sales order from the list to understand the underlying details.

Fig 7: Insight Card "Delivery Block"Fig 8: Remove Delivery BlockFig 9 : Delivery Block Removed

 From the "Insight Card," I swiftly transition to the sales order and discover it is in a blocked status. Upon comprehensive examination, I remove the block and allow it to proceed further.

Fig 10: Updated insight card

Upon returning to the "Home Page," the affected sales order is no longer visible in the insight card.

Now I subsequently return to the homepage. I  have a customer meeting scheduled for later this week. I seek a summary of recent customer activities and an analysis of significant financial transactions and contracts in preparation for the meeting. I use Customer 360 to obtain this information, providing me with a comprehensive overview for the customer.

Fig 11: Customer 360Fig 12 : Customer 360 : Business TransactionsFig 13: Customer 360 : All Transaction view

I intend to verify specific customer facts through the "Customer 360 View." I leverage "Smart Filtering" to identify specific customers from an extensive list. All consumer details are now before me. I can examine every minute detail of it. I utilize “AI—Smart Summarization” to extract all important facts about that customer.  I want to communicate this information to the  management for weekly meeting.

Fig 14 : AI summary for productFig 15: AI summary for Sales

A customer contacts me regarding an inquiry concerning a sales order, providing the "customer reference" number. (It is ZXW-560 in this case.) I can utilize the launchpad search within an application to promptly locate the business object.

Fig 16: Customer ReferenceFig 17: Result of Customer reference

The system retrieves the precise order through efficient search and presents it for inspection.

For my upcoming task, I intend to seek assistance from the AI-powered chatbot named "Joule." I am mostly engaged in sales activities, but due to the sick leave of the return clerk , I have to take care of that part too. I receive a return from a customer but lack familiarity with the return creation procedure. I use Joule to ascertain the method for facilitating a return effortlessly.

Fig 18: JouleFig 19: Joule for Customer returnFig 20: Create Return

I have initiated a chat with "Joule". I seek assistance with "return order management" and am requesting guidance from "Joule." Joule is providing comprehensive information, including the application name, direct navigation, and assistance features. I have accessed "Manage Customer Return" through direct navigation from Joule.

However, there are numerous fields that remain unfamiliar to me. I request assistance from "X-Ray" to comprehend certain fields and their values. Subsequently, I observe that the system has initiated AI-driven auto-recommendations for return orders. This feature facilitates a seamless return creation process.

Fig 21: Help recomendationFig 22: X Ray helpFig 22: Auto RecomendationFig 23: Choose RecomendationFig 24: Save & Release

Upon the creation of a return order, I wish to pass on its data via Microsoft Teams to another team member for subsequent processing. The returns clerk receives a Microsoft Teams notice on their mobile device for the next action.

Fig 25 : Share in Microsoft TeamsFig 26: Display in MS card

This step concludes the system navigation/demo. 

SAP S/4HANA tackles these problems directly:
You can see your clients from every angle using SAP S/4HANA, which is great for CRM (customer relationship management). You may better comprehend their requirements, communicate with them on a personal level, and develop more profound relationships.
2. Sales Performance Tracking: With the platform's real-time analytics and reporting, you can keep tabs on how well you're doing compared to your goals. You can quickly pinpoint problem areas and implement solutions with this level of transparency.
3. Up-to-Date Market Trends and Insights: The business AI in SAP S/4HANA is always analyzing market data to bring you the most recent trends and insights. If you want to keep ahead of the competition and adjust your sales tactics to fit the ever-changing industry, you need this information.

Advantages for Clients
Customers, not just salespeople, may reap the benefits of SAP S/4HANA. Customers are guaranteed fast and personalized service with SAP S/4HANA's help, thanks to its ability to streamline sales processes and enhance efficiency. The software keeps clients informed about the status of their orders and any potential concerns through real-time updates and analytics. This openness cultivates trust and encourages loyal patronage in the long run.

An epic shift for SAP business artificial intelligence (AI), intuitive user interface (UI), and digital assistant Joule all come together in SAP S/4HANA, making it a revolutionary product in the corporate software industry. A key differentiator for SAP S/4HANA is its ability to alleviate sales agents' problems while simultaneously increasing client happiness. Salespeople can remain busy, educated, and responsive because to the platform's easy design and powerful features, which drive corporate success.
To sum up, SAP S/4HANA is revolutionizing the day-to-day work of sales reps. Its digital assistant Joule, user-friendly UI, and incorporation of business AI make it an essential tool for sales teams. Sales agents work more efficiently with SAP S/4HANA, and the company solidifies its position as an industry leader in enterprise software by alleviating typical business pain points and increasing customer satisfaction. Without a question, SAP S/4HANA will be pivotal in determining how sales operations are shaped in the future as firms undergo continuous evolution.

entry.title: Re: Smooth Transition Ahead: SAP Enable Now Migration from SAP BTP Neo to SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
entry.content:
Hi @Maggy , 

all the available information is listed and linked in the blog above. You can support by guiding the customers through the preparation and testing steps outlined in the blog and the linked KBAs. There's currently no separate newsletter, but automatic emails will be sent out. 

Yes, all existing SAP Enable Now, cloud edition customers will be impacted. 

Please make sure you're following the Consultant Workzone hosted by Mike Fritz (invitation on request) to be up to date. 

Take care,

Kristina

entry.title: Re: Send an email with a screenshot of my Service Quotation
entry.content:
Hello,

To send an automatic screenshot of the app by email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, you may need to utilize custom solution approaches as there is no direct feature that accomplishes this within standard SAP functionalities.

One potential approach is to use SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation (RPA) Create Business Partner in SAP S/4HANA Cloud using… in conjunction with SAP Workflow Service to orchestrate a process where a bot can take a screenshot of the current app and attach it to an email. These technologies allow for the automation of specific tasks, such as interacting with an app screen, capturing the current view, and sending emails, which can be configured to suit your needs.

For SAP Intelligent RPA, you would configure a project where the bot is instructed to take screenshots at desired intervals or on specific triggers. Then, you can program it to send these screenshots via email automatically using SAP Workflow, which orchestrates the process of approval, email sending, and task tracking.

In scenarios where you’re focused on collaborative features, SAP Collaboration Manager allows users to take screenshots and annotate them directly within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition and send them via email through integrated channels or utilizing Microsoft Teams for sharing.

To implement this:

  1. Setup SAP Intelligent RPA to capture screenshots and configure bots for sending emails.
  2. Configure SAP Workflow Service to manage the workflow of capturing and emailing screenshots.
  3. Utilize SAP Collaboration Manager How to Display Situations in Your Custom Apps with… for quick integration with email and collaborative platforms.

This approach leverages existing SAP solutions and requires some setup and possibly collaboration with SAP partners or consultants to tailor the solution to your specific processes.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: why sales order can still be changed after created bi...
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, it is indeed possible to change certain information in sales orders even after a billing document has been created. Usually, business processes are designed to be flexible to accommodate various commercial necessities. The ability to adjust material quantities, both increased and decreased, reflects this flexibility.

Key Aspects:

  • Material Quantity Modification: Users are able to modify the material quantity in both directions post-billing because SAP caters to dynamic business environments Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2302 where order modifications might be required due to changes in demand, supply, or other logistic considerations.

  • Incoterms and PO Number: These can also be adjusted to reflect changes in delivery terms or fulfillment requirements.

This characteristic of SAP S/4HANA Cloud ensures that businesses maintain agility in their operations, allowing them to quickly respond to changes and maintain customer satisfaction.

Recommendations

While the behavior you’ve observed is normal, it’s advisable to implement controls or approvals for changes that significantly impact logistics or finances to ensure proper auditing and monitoring.

For further details, you may explore SAP’s documentation on role-based access and sales order management Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Sales processes, which can help in setting up restrictions and controls based on business roles.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Impossibile to deliver CBFD Deliv.Free of Charge and...
entry.content:
Hello,

When dealing with sales orders of type CBFD (Delivery Free of Charge) and SD2 (Replacement Delivery Order), you may encounter issues where these orders do not appear or are not processed correctly due to stock-related challenges.

Solutions

  1. Correct Application Usage:

    • For CBFD - Delivery Free of Charge orders, ensure you are using the correct application, such as ‘Manage Sales Orders Without Charge’ (App ID F2305) 2796935 - [Public Could] Sales Orders of type CBFD - Deliv.Free of charge are not visible in the Manage Sales Orders App (App ID F1873) instead of the standard ‘Manage Sales Orders’ app (App ID F1873).

  2. Check Material Availability:

    • Perform an availability check on your material using SAP’s availability checking features. This will help you identify any missing components that might be causing the issue. You may need to bring the missing materials into stock.

  3. Stock Transfer or Purchase Order:

    • If the required stock for fulfilling these orders is not available, consider creating stock transport orders or purchase orders to replenish the stock.

  4. Technical Setup Verification:

    • Ensure that your SAP system is configured correctly concerning stock, delivery, and order types. Sometimes, issues may arise due to misconfigured setups.

Considerations

  • Make sure the material numbers and plant codes are correctly assigned to the orders and are consistent with the stock held in your system.
  • If stock is still unavailable after internal checks and setups, cross-verify if there are any stock reservations or allocations that are preventing the fulfillment of these orders.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Lluis Salvador
entry.content:
It’s been a real honor to receive the #BIF from @Raquel_Pereira_da_Cunha , one of my first mentors in Solution Manager (#solman) and a great friend, despite the distance. Thank you so much, Raquel

Let's start with a brief introduction
My real name is Luis but everyone in SAP world know me as Lluis, I break #SAP solutions by day, build community by night, and somewhere in between, I clean corruption in Viladecans city 😁.

I’m a naturally curious person who loves understanding how things work and figuring out how we can make them better. These days, my biggest passions revolve around technology, teamwork, and building strong communities.

Professionally, I began my journey as an #ABAP developer back in early 2004. Since then, I’ve moved through several areas like SAP Solution Manager, SAP Support processes, SAP PartnerEdge, Enable Now, Tricentis, Cloud ALM and now discovering WalkME. I’m lucky to work with a dedicated and talented team, helping companies improve their processes with #ALM solutions. Solving tough challenges is something I genuinely enjoy.

After that #ABAP phase, one day I suddenly found myself in a Support/Solution Manager team, working on a task requested by SAP. With barely any information and a massive volume of data. I started asking questions in the old SCN forums. I really asked a lot,a lot..., and got a huge amount of help from the SAP Communitty.
Step by step, I started meeting great people and true SAP experts. The first ones who really made an impact were @Raquel_Pereira_da_Cunha, Wences @wlacaze , @TomCenens  ,and @Vivek_Hegde  .
They just opened the doors of the SAP Community for me. I met most of them later at SAP events, and many more great people inside the communitty, and thanks to their support, I kept connecting with more experts and incredible SAP influencers.

That experience helped me grow both technically and personally. It even took me to Manila in the Philippines to conduct an on-site PCOE audit, and later to Argentina to launch an international collaboration for PCE Support Services, all while running various projects with #solman at the core. It also motivated me to contribute back to the SAP Community as much as my time allows.

From ManillaTaking Lunch in ZambiaTo Argentina
The best place to atten an on-site Audit from #SAPFirst Solution Manager in Zambia (Africa)Where the International journey of a global PCOE started

I’d also like to say a few heartfelt words to @david_ruizb , @enric101 , Imma, Losel @starverso, and of course, my lovely Mireia. You’re more than just friends, you’re part of a little family built around a shared passion called SAP Inside Track Barcelona. Year after year, #SITBCN has grown into something truly special, and it wouldn’t be the same without each of you. I’m especially grateful to @david_ruizb  for opening the door and inviting me into this incredible community. Thanks to that, I’ve not only learned and grown professionally, but I’ve also found people who inspire me and make this journey so much more meaning.

Outside of work, I love diving into new technologies and ideas, one of my current obsessions is exploring how local governments could embrace decentralization and community-driven innovation. That’s what led me to create The WatchCans, a local initiative designed to bring neighbors together to spark change in our city through creative and fun quests.

At the end of the day, I’m just someone who enjoys learning new things, connecting people, and experimenting with tech projects, while also relaxing with some good old-school trading card games as a classic UW control player. During the pandemic, I got curious about #smartcities technologies expecting flying cars and AI-powered recycling bins. But what I found instead was... a black hole. A swirl of flashy PowerPoint decks, endless buzzwords like “sustainability,” “resilience,” and “digitalization,” and public funds mysteriously disappearing into pilot programs that no one remembered six months late. In the end, most of those initiatives seemed less about real impact and more about fueling political narratives, just rebooting the same cycle of “innovation” meant to “improve” quality of life, without ever addressing the real issues on the ground.

What I thought would be a tech-driven journey into the future became a crash course in how local governments often miss the mark. That wake-up call pulled me closer to municipal politics and eventually led me to create a citizen-led network, an online space where neighbors who genuinely care about their city can take real, collective action.
Nowadays, we need technology more than ever but we also need to make it human, accessible, and rooted in purpose. Not just another platform for professionals, but a tool that empowers everyone; I hope the SAP can affort this in the future. In the end, I came to a simple but powerful conclusion, the smartest thing a city can invest in isn’t a new gadget or app it’s in its people. Empowered, connected, and willing to collaborate for better local policies…

'It Is simply people who are the real drivers of lasting change.'

Here are a couple of photos showing a few more hobbies that, from time to time, help me disconnect from all the SAP overload, non-SAP, non-Viladecans moments:

Magic The Gathering trading card games offer a great escape from real life, a moment to immerse yourself in a fantasy world of strategy and tactics that keeps your mind sharp without needing to us /hNot as much as I’d like due to my current physical condition, but hiking snowy mountains is where you truly feel alone with nature, with no other goal than reaching the end of the trail and pushing your mental limits.

Following the previous question, I’d like to share some AI-generated images that more or less capture how I’ve discovered the true power of the SAP community over the years where I can say that I really enjoy this journey, just meeting people in real live.

Let's go for BIF questions

If you were 20 again, what would you study?
It’s funny to say I might be stretching the limits of this question, but after 21 years working with SAP, I can confidently say I’d still choose to study computer science and work as a SAP consultant. Having the chance to be involved in big business decisions has been incredibly enriching and fulfilling.

What is the best lesson your parents taught you?
Sadly, I didn’t have the chance to learn many formal lessons from my parents, but something I’ll never forget is the focus and energy they put into fighting for their goals, even when life wasn’t on their side. No one else has shown me that kind of consistency and determination to keep pushing forward and pursue their life purpose, no matter the obstacles.

What do you enjoy most in your work and why?
One of the things I enjoy most about my current job is growing alongside a team, facing challenges together, working on different projects, and sharing a common goal. Over the years, I’ve had the chance to grow with amazing colleagues, some of them are still part of my team, others have moved on, but each of them, in their own way, brought valuable experiences that helped me see things from new perspectives and learn new skills.

Share an interesting/funny story related to your product
A very funny thing about working with SAP is that nothing is really impossible; everything is technically doable if your imagination is wild enough… and directly proportional to the amount of custom code you’re willing to write. 😄
A few years ago, someone told us that in a single Solution Manager instance, we’d only be able to manage around 10 customers as a VAR partner. Sounds reasonable, right ? Well, we ended up onboarding more than 300 customers' SAP infraestructture , it worked smoothly, and, with not too much custom code.
Now the question is… will we be able to pull off something similar in #Cloud ALM ? Challenge accepted.😉

Recommend resources of information that helped you in your work
If anyone would ask me about about a great resource, definitely I will recommend SAP Community, there is any resource better than this can put you in contact with the correct person when you need it, you can learn with SAP Learning HUB, you can get professional support from SAP Services, you can know people through social media, but the big one is definitely SAP Community; live is all about you contact networks, SAP Community must be the main branch of it.

What do you imagine or predict is the future of your topic? (Mobile, Cloud etc., be creative be daring, be entertaining)
I’m not really a Nostradamus when it comes to predictions, but there’s something I’ve been thinking about lately. With all the progress around #AI, I have the feeling that something big is coming, something we’re not seeing clearly yet… and maybe we won’t until it’s already here.
Right now, I still see a lot of companies needing classic developers. There’s tons of custom code out there, and adapting it to fit business processes is still part of our daily work. But let’s be honest, this won’t last forever. #AI is learning fast, and it's not just about writing code or writing e-mail, it’s starting to understand patterns, generate solutions, and even make decisions or take actions.
I see tools today that can do in minutes what used to take us hours. And while that’s amazing, it also makes me wonder where does that leave us as professionals ? Or even more, as humans ?
I think we’re heading toward a time where technical skills alone won’t be enough. What will matter most is our ability to adapt, to connect with others, to bring creativity, empathy, and real value beyond just executing tasks.
And yeah… in a future full of #AI agents and robots taking  control of the citizens, I won’t lie. I’m a bit worried 😅
But I also believe that if we stay curious, keep learning, and focus on what makes us human, we’ll still have a very important role to play following #SAP strategy … 

This is a great example of how we support each other in the SAP Community, even when we get blisters from walking around Heidelberg streets 🙂

To continue with the #BIF, I’d like to mention two people I really appreciate, even if we’re far apart one is @DanielMateo and the other is @Shiroh44

I’ve had the chance to meet Dani in person and share experiences with him, and as a professional, I’d like to ask him this question: "How do you survive when SAP keeps shifting our journey to new products?"
Because let’s be honest, keeping up with SAP’s evolution isn’t always easy !		On the other hand, I’m also very happy to mention Shiro from #Japan. Even though we’ve never met in person, I’ve had the chance to connect with him through social media and some SAP communitty events. It’s amazing how SAP brings people together from all over the world, it's not just about Europe, and I’m genuinely curious to hear the kind of insights Shiro can share from his experience in Japan with SAP
@DanielMateo @Shiroh44

I’m truly honored to be part of this community now as a little Champion. Huge thanks to @NilPeksen for thinking of me and to everyone behind the scenes or cheering quietly, your support doesn’t go unno.
Thanks for this opportunity to reflect and share part of my journey. #SAPhas given me not just a career, but also a global community and that’s something truly valuable.

Whether it’s through #SAP, or just helping a neighbor debug life, I’ll keep doing what I do best, learning, sharing, and having fun along the way.

Looking forward to reading the next stories in this Blog It Forward chains.

entry.title: Re: 关于 SAP 公有云（S/4HANA Cloud）的销售数据锁定
entry.content:
您好，

在SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) 中，为了在某个特定时间段内自动锁定销售数据的创建和修改，可以考虑以下方法：

使用业务锁定功能

在SAP系统中，业务锁定是常用的方法，此功能允许您锁定特定组件或模块，在指定锁定期间限制用户操作。

锁定步骤

  1. 配置模块锁定:

    • 您可以创建工作流事件或配置锁定业务交易代码（如VA01, VA02, VF01）。
    • 配置业务锁定以避免用户在指定的时间段内进行不必要的操作。

  2. 使用工作日历:

    • 创建工作日历以规定特定时间段内事务处理的限制。
    • 设置定期的工作日历来限制每月1日至3日的销售单据的创建和修改。

财务结账控制

对于财务结账期间的实施，确保财务凭证的锁定时间与业务锁定时间分开控制，可以通过以下方式进行细分设置。

锁定操作：

  • 期限锁定：通常使用’锁定期间的物料过账日期’，确保财务在规定期间后的正常过账不受影响。
  • 单据锁定：设置条件以防止在月结期间的物料单据创建、修改。

通过上述的锁定策略，确保在汇总期间和财务结账期间对销售数据的创建和修改进行自动控制，避免汇总结果受到人为干扰。

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Document Number Range Issue for Prior Year Posting – SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
We are facing an issue in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud related to document number range while posting Journal Entries for the previous Financial Year (2023-24).

→ Document Type: [Mention Document Type]
→ Number Range for FY 2023-24: Starts from 87
→ Number Range for FY 2024-25: Starts from 14

Issue:
When we post Journal Entries for FY 2023-24, the system is wrongly picking the current year number range starting from 14 instead of continuing from 87. Earlier, before opening the previous year period, it was correctly picking 87 series.

Request:
Kindly check and help us to correct this issue so that:

  • FY 2023-24 postings pick number range 87

  • FY 2024-25 postings pick number range 14
